title: Six-strand challah
subtitle: Plaited egg-enriched Jewish Sabbath bread
excerpt: A six-strand plaited loaf enriched with eggs and oil rather than butter, with a mahogany glaze and a pillowy, slightly sweet crumb. The six-strand braid produces a distinctive raised crest along the top.
sourceNotes: Challah is a Shabbat and holiday bread with roots in Ashkenazi Jewish tradition from at least the sixteenth century. The six-strand plait is the classic Shabbat form; the round Rosh Hashanah loaf is a separate shape. The egg-and-oil enrichment (no dairy, to keep the bread pareve) and the egg-wash glaze are defining characteristics. Hannah Glasse, The Art of Cookery (1747), Project Gutenberg, enriched plaited loaves appear as a variant of 'fine manchet' in the bread chapter.

content.text: Challah is enriched with eggs and oil, never butter, keeping it

so it can be served at a meal that includes meat. The six strands produce a domed, crested loaf that slices cleanly and pulls apart in soft, eggy layers. The glaze is applied twice (once before proving and once just before it goes in the oven), which gives the deep mahogany colour.

The six-strand plait looks involved but follows a single repeated move. Number the strands 1 to 6 left to right at the start, then follow this pattern: 1 over 6, 2 over 1, 6 over 2. Repeat from the new position until the dough runs out. Pinch and tuck the ends under.

attrs.prepNote: neutral, light olive oil or sunflower

content.text: Combine {{strong-bread-flour}}, {{yeast-fast-action}}, {{salt-table}}, and {{caster-sugar}}. Beat 2 of the {{eggs}} lightly and add with the {{olive-oil}} and {{water}}. Mix to a soft, slightly sticky dough. Knead for 10 minutes until very smooth and elastic, enriched doughs need extra attention here to develop the gluten around the eggs and oil. The dough should pull away from your hands cleanly when you flick your wrists.
Place in an oiled bowl, cover, and leave until doubled, about 1 hour.

Divide the dough into 6 equal pieces. Roll each into a strand about 45 cm long, thicker in the middle and tapered at the ends. Line them up side by side and press the tops firmly together. Plait using the 1-over-6, 2-over-1, 6-over-2 sequence, repeating until the strands run out. Press and tuck the bottom ends under. Transfer to a lined baking tray. Beat the remaining egg and brush lightly over the whole loaf. Cover loosely and prove for 45 minutes until visibly puffed. Heat the oven to 190°C (170°C fan).

Brush the loaf again with egg wash just before it goes in. Bake for 28 to 30 minutes until deep mahogany and hollow-sounding when tapped underneath. An instant-read thermometer through the side should read 93 to 95°C. Cool completely on a wire rack before cutting, the enriched crumb needs to set.
